Start wi da softest yarn ye can find, maybe frae sheep grazed on da isles, an choose colours dat tell da story o da land—deep blues for da sea, whites for da winter snows, an reds for da sunset. Cast on wi care, so da stitches lie even, an feel da weight o da wool in yer hands, like da auld knitters did. Work da pattern gentle, so da motifs o da waves an da flowers come clear, an listen tae da wind tae tell when tae turn. As ye knit, whisper da names o da stitches tae yersel—‘knit,’ ‘purl,’ ‘cable’—like a charm tae keep da pattern true.
